title: Chunk index entry
description: One JSON object per line in `knowledge/processed/chunks.jsonl`. Embeddings live alongside in `embeddings.npy` (same row order).

source_id.description: Source manifest ID (e.g. `S001`, `SRC-022`). Maps the chunk back to its license metadata.

rule_id.description: When the chunk is a jsonl rule, the originating `Rule_ID` (e.g. `R001`, `R-005`).

source_path.description: Absolute or repo-relative path to the source file.

text.description: The chunk body, after normalisation (HTML tags stripped, etc.). Used for retrieval scoring.

title.description: Best-effort heading or title (from `Title:` in source manifest, from `#` in markdown, etc.).

allowed_use.description: Copied from the source manifest. Drives `redact_for_llm` policy.

summary.description: Our own short summary for LLM exposure (the field that gets sent through redaction).

checksum.description: SHA-256 of the source file at index time. Lets `knowledge index` skip unchanged files on rebuild.
